sources: NHMRC / NZ MoH Nutrient Reference Values for Australia and New Zealand (2006; sodium and fluoride revised 2017)
Food and Nutrition Board IOM/NAS Dietary Reference Intakes - Vitamins, Elements and Macronutrients tables, males 19-30
FSANZ and NZ Medsafe for vitamin K and vitamin E ANZ values
notes: Every target is verified against a named source except omega3LC.

Where Australia and the US disagree, the note field records both. Australian figures are used for zinc, vitamin C, vitamin E and vitamin K (verified); US figures for phosphorus, selenium, copper, manganese and pantothenic acid.
If you are still 18, the 14-18 band applies: calcium 1300 mg, phosphorus 1250 mg, iron 11 mg, zinc 11 mg, vitamin C 75 mg, vitamin K 75 ug.
NHMRC has draft updated NRVs in consultation; final publication expected during 2026.
targetMin/targetMax are only present where a published range exists (currently the AMDR for carbs and fat). Never invent a band by scaling the target - absence of a bound is information.
upperLimit added for 16 nutrients. All are 2.2x their target or higher, so diet alone will not approach them - they matter only if supplements are introduced.

alertRules.notes: A day qualifies for a nutrient only if coverage >= minCoveragePct and entry confidence >= minConfidence. Non-qualifying days are 'unknown' - they neither extend nor break a run. Alert fires when qualifyingDaysRequired qualifying days inside the trailing windowDays breach the threshold in that nutrient's direction. Nutrients whose targetConfidence is not in allowedTargetConfidence display but never alert. Nutrients may carry targetMin and targetMax defining an acceptable band. Where both exist, breaching below targetMin or above targetMax counts toward a streak; inside the band is satisfied. A null targetMin means no meaningful lower bound exists.
alertRules.upperLimitRule: upperLimit is the Tolerable Upper Intake Level - a safety ceiling, distinct from targetMax which is a dietary band. Breaching it fires immediately on a single qualifying day; it does not use the streak window. A null upperLimit means no ceiling exists and none must be inferred.

energy.label: Energy
energy.source: NRV EER 10.8-13.8 MJ/day for a 76 kg male 19-30
energy.note: Starting point. Adjust by actual weight trend over 3-4 weeks, not by feel. No lower bound set: any figure would be invented. Adjust the target itself off your weight trend.

protein.label: Protein
protein.note: NRV RDI is 64 g - that is the deficiency floor. 150 g is the hypertrophy target for 75 kg.

carbs.label: Carbohydrate
carbs.source: AMDR 45-65% of energy (US DRI macronutrient table, verified); bounds derived from your 3100 kcal target

fat.label: Fat
fat.source: AMDR 20-35% of energy (US DRI macronutrient table, verified); bounds derived from your 3100 kcal target

fibre.label: Fibre
fibre.source: US DRI, Food and Nutrition Board IOM/NAS, males 19-30 (verified); also the Australian Suggested Dietary Target

cholesterol.label: Cholesterol
cholesterol.source: NHMRC reviewed cholesterol and set no NRV

water.label: Water (total)
water.source: US DRI, Food and Nutrition Board IOM/NAS, males 19-30 (verified), total water incl. food

alcohol.label: Alcohol
alcohol.source: NHMRC 2020: max 4 in a day, 10 per week

omega3ALA.label: Omega-3 (ALA)
omega3ALA.source: US DRI, Food and Nutrition Board IOM/NAS, males 19-30 (verified)

omega6LA.label: Omega-6 (LA)
omega6LA.source: US DRI, Food and Nutrition Board IOM/NAS, males 19-30 (verified)

omega3LC.label: Omega-3 (EPA+DHA+DPA)
omega3LC.source: Australian Suggested Dietary Target - not verified; no US AI exists

calcium.label: Calcium
calcium.source: US DRI, Food and Nutrition Board IOM/NAS, males 19-30 (verified); AU agrees
calcium.upperLimitSource: US DRI Tolerable Upper Intake Levels, Elements table (IOM/NAS), 19-70y (verified)

phosphorus.label: Phosphorus
phosphorus.source: US DRI, Food and Nutrition Board IOM/NAS, males 19-30 (verified)
phosphorus.note: Australia sets 1000 mg. US figure used because it is verified.
phosphorus.upperLimitSource: US DRI Tolerable Upper Intake Levels, Elements table (IOM/NAS), 19-70y (verified)

magnesium.label: Magnesium
magnesium.source: US DRI, Food and Nutrition Board IOM/NAS, males 19-30 (verified); AU agrees
magnesium.upperLimitNote: US UL is 350 mg but applies to magnesium from supplements ONLY, not food. That is below the 400 mg food target, so it cannot be used as a ceiling. Deliberately null.

iron.label: Iron
iron.source: NHMRC/NZ MoH NRV (verified) RDI 8 mg, EAR 6 mg; US agrees
iron.upperLimitSource: US DRI Tolerable Upper Intake Levels, Elements table (IOM/NAS), 19-70y (verified)

zinc.label: Zinc
zinc.source: NHMRC/NZ MoH NRV (verified) RDI 14 mg, EAR 12 mg
zinc.note: US RDA is 11 mg. Australian figure used - it is verified and higher.
zinc.upperLimitSource: US DRI Tolerable Upper Intake Levels, Elements table (IOM/NAS), 19-70y (verified)
zinc.upperLimitNote: Closest ceiling to its target of any nutrient here (2.9x). A zinc supplement on top of a high-meat diet is the realistic way to approach it.

iodine.label: Iodine
iodine.source: US DRI, Food and Nutrition Board IOM/NAS, males 19-30 (verified); AU agrees
iodine.upperLimitSource: US DRI Tolerable Upper Intake Levels, Elements table (IOM/NAS), 19-70y (verified)

selenium.label: Selenium
selenium.source: US DRI, Food and Nutrition Board IOM/NAS, males 19-30 (verified)
selenium.upperLimitSource: US DRI Tolerable Upper Intake Levels, Elements table (IOM/NAS), 19-70y (verified)

copper.label: Copper
copper.source: US DRI, Food and Nutrition Board IOM/NAS, males 19-30 (verified)
copper.note: Australia sets 1.7 mg - a large gap. US figure used because it is verified.
copper.upperLimitSource: US DRI Tolerable Upper Intake Levels, Elements table (IOM/NAS), 19-70y (verified)

manganese.label: Manganese
manganese.source: US DRI, Food and Nutrition Board IOM/NAS, males 19-30 (verified)
manganese.upperLimitSource: US DRI Tolerable Upper Intake Levels, Elements table (IOM/NAS), 19-70y (verified)

chromium.label: Chromium
chromium.source: US DRI, Food and Nutrition Board IOM/NAS, males 19-30 (verified); AU agrees
chromium.upperLimitNote: No UL established (not determinable from available data). Absence is a finding, not a gap.

molybdenum.label: Molybdenum
molybdenum.source: US DRI, Food and Nutrition Board IOM/NAS, males 19-30 (verified); AU agrees
molybdenum.upperLimitSource: US DRI Tolerable Upper Intake Levels, Elements table (IOM/NAS), 19-70y (verified)

potassium.label: Potassium
potassium.note: Authorities disagree badly here: FDA 4700, AU AI 3800, US DRI 2019 revised to 3400. At 4700 this will flag often - most people fall short. Consider dropping to 3800.
potassium.upperLimitNote: No UL established (not determinable from available data). Absence is a finding, not a gap.

sodium.label: Sodium
sodium.note: Australian Suggested Dietary Target is stricter at 2000 mg.

vitaminA.label: Vitamin A
vitaminA.source: US DRI, Food and Nutrition Board IOM/NAS, males 19-30 (verified); AU agrees
vitaminA.upperLimitSource: US DRI Tolerable Upper Intake Levels, Vitamins (IOM/NAS), adults (verified)
vitaminA.upperLimitNote: Preformed vitamin A only. Beta-carotene from food is not subject to this.

thiamin.label: Thiamin (B1)
thiamin.source: US DRI, Food and Nutrition Board IOM/NAS, males 19-30 (verified); AU agrees
thiamin.upperLimitNote: No UL established (not determinable from available data). Absence is a finding, not a gap.

riboflavin.label: Riboflavin (B2)
riboflavin.source: US DRI, Food and Nutrition Board IOM/NAS, males 19-30 (verified); AU agrees
riboflavin.upperLimitNote: No UL established (not determinable from available data). Absence is a finding, not a gap.

niacin.label: Niacin (B3)
niacin.source: US DRI, Food and Nutrition Board IOM/NAS, males 19-30 (verified); AU agrees
niacin.upperLimitSource: US DRI Tolerable Upper Intake Levels, Vitamins (IOM/NAS), adults (verified)
niacin.upperLimitNote: Applies to nicotinic acid from supplements and fortified food, not niacin occurring in food.

vitaminB6.label: Vitamin B6
vitaminB6.source: US DRI, Food and Nutrition Board IOM/NAS, males 19-30 (verified); AU agrees
vitaminB6.upperLimitSource: US DRI Tolerable Upper Intake Levels, Vitamins (IOM/NAS), adults (verified)
vitaminB6.upperLimitNote: US figure. Australia sets 50 mg and EFSA revised to 12 mg in 2023 - wide disagreement, treat as soft.

vitaminB12.label: Vitamin B12
vitaminB12.source: US DRI, Food and Nutrition Board IOM/NAS, males 19-30 (verified); AU agrees
vitaminB12.upperLimitNote: No UL established (not determinable from available data). Absence is a finding, not a gap.

folate.label: Folate
folate.source: US DRI, Food and Nutrition Board IOM/NAS, males 19-30 (verified); AU agrees
folate.upperLimitSource: US DRI Tolerable Upper Intake Levels, Vitamins (IOM/NAS), adults (verified)
folate.upperLimitNote: Applies to folic acid from supplements and fortified food, not food folate.

pantothenic.label: Pantothenic acid (B5)
pantothenic.source: US DRI, Food and Nutrition Board IOM/NAS, males 19-30 (verified)
pantothenic.upperLimitNote: No UL established (not determinable from available data). Absence is a finding, not a gap.

biotin.label: Biotin (B7)
biotin.source: US DRI, Food and Nutrition Board IOM/NAS, males 19-30 (verified); AU agrees
biotin.upperLimitNote: No UL established (not determinable from available data). Absence is a finding, not a gap.

choline.label: Choline
choline.source: US DRI, Food and Nutrition Board IOM/NAS, males 19-30 (verified); AU agrees
choline.upperLimitNote: A UL exists but was not verified in this pass. Left null rather than guessed.

vitaminC.label: Vitamin C
vitaminC.source: NHMRC/NZ MoH NRV (verified) RDI 45 mg, EAR 30 mg
vitaminC.note: US RDA is 90 mg. Australian figure used - verified directly from the NRV rationale.
vitaminC.upperLimitSource: US DRI Tolerable Upper Intake Levels, Vitamins (IOM/NAS), adults (verified)
vitaminC.upperLimitNote: US UL. Australia sets no UL for vitamin C but names 1000 mg/day as a prudent limit - the stricter figure.

vitaminD.label: Vitamin D
vitaminD.source: US DRI, Food and Nutrition Board IOM/NAS, males 19-30 (verified) 2004 table; AU still uses 5 ug
vitaminD.note: Assumes sun exposure. Most vitamin D is not dietary, so a low reading here means little. US revised to 15 ug in 2011; Australia did not.
vitaminD.upperLimitSource: US DRI Tolerable Upper Intake Levels, Vitamins (IOM/NAS), adults (verified)
vitaminD.upperLimitNote: 1997/2000 IOM figure. The 2011 US review raised this to 100 ug; Australia did not follow. Lower value kept.

vitaminE.label: Vitamin E
vitaminE.source: NZ Medsafe, shared ANZ NRV: 10 mg men / 7 mg women
vitaminE.upperLimitSource: US DRI Tolerable Upper Intake Levels, Vitamins (IOM/NAS), adults (verified)
vitaminE.upperLimitNote: Applies to supplemental alpha-tocopherol, not food.

vitaminK.label: Vitamin K
vitaminK.source: FSANZ: ANZ Adequate Intake 70 ug men / 60 ug women
vitaminK.upperLimitNote: No UL established (not determinable from available data). Absence is a finding, not a gap.

entries.item: Homemade double espresso latte (regular milk, 1 tsp sugar)
Ground beef, onion, tomato paste, salt, pepper
Meredith Dairy goat cheese (marinated, in oil)
entries.qty: ~100g cheese + oil, estimated 'a block' from jar

entries.item: Bagel, cream cheese (heaped) and blueberry fennel compote — Jersey Cafe, Woollahra Sydney
